name: Create and Run a Workflow
query: Create a workflow that fetches data from an API and processes it, then run it
expected_behavior: Step 1: Check if `conductor` CLI is available by running `conductor --version`
Step 2: Verify server is reachable (CLI auto-detects local server, or uses CONDUCTOR_SERVER_URL for remote)
Step 3: Consult references/workflow-definition.md for JSON schema and task types
Step 4: Construct a workflow definition JSON with HTTP task for API fetch and SIMPLE task for processing
Step 5: Include proper inputParameters with ${...} expressions to pass data between tasks
Step 6: Write the workflow JSON to a file
Step 7: Register the workflow using `conductor workflow create workflow.json`
Step 8: List task definitions with `conductor taskDef list` to check if SIMPLE tasks have registered workers
Step 9: If any SIMPLE task has no task definition, offer to create the task definition and scaffold a worker
Step 10: Start the workflow using `conductor workflow start -w <name> -i '{...}'`
Step 11: Check execution status using `conductor workflow get-execution <workflowId>`
Step 12: Report the execution status, workflowId, and any task failures to the user
success_criteria: Workflow definition follows the schema from references/workflow-definition.md
Workflow includes at least two tasks with correct types (HTTP, SIMPLE)
Tasks use ${...} expressions to wire inputs/outputs between them
Any $.varName referenced in JavaScript scripts (INLINE expression, DO_WHILE loopCondition, SWITCH javascript expression) is declared as an inputParameters key on that task
Workflow is registered before being started (create before start)
SIMPLE tasks are checked against task definitions before starting the workflow
Missing workers are flagged and user is offered to scaffold one
CLI commands are preferred; fallback script used if CLI unavailable
Execution ID is captured from the start command and used for status check
Final output includes workflowId, status, and task details
Auth tokens are never echoed in output
